sources.title: Common Sense
sources.citation: Paine, Thomas. Common Sense. Philadelphia, 1776. Project Gutenberg EBook #147. https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/147
sources.rights_rationale: Published 1776; author died 1809. Well outside any copyright term in any jurisdiction. Project Gutenberg hosts a clean plain-text edition under its standard public-domain release. No OCR required.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Download plain-text UTF-8 from https://www.gutenberg.org/cache/epub/147/pg147.txt. Strip Gutenberg header/footer boilerplate before storing. Save to expected_local_path.
sources.notes: Core revolutionary-break document. High density of liberty/tyranny and founding-sacrifice metaphors. Essential for cross-case comparison with Lincoln covenant language.

sources.title: Declaration of Independence and Jefferson draft materials
sources.citation: Declaration of Independence, July 4, 1776. National Archives. https://www.archives.gov/founding-docs/declaration-transcript. Jefferson draft: Founders Online, National Archives, https://founders.archives.gov/
sources.rights_rationale: U.S. federal government document, 1776. No copyright applies under any U.S. or international standard. Multiple authoritative clean-text sources available from National Archives and Founders Online.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Transcription text available directly from National Archives at https://www.archives.gov/founding-docs/declaration-transcript. Jefferson's rough draft and committee draft are at https://founders.archives.gov/documents/Jefferson/01-01-02-0176. Copy clean text; preserve section structure. Save final and draft versions as separate files if Jefferson draft is included.
sources.notes: Final Declaration plus Jefferson draft preferred for showing editorial suppression and substitution of sacrifice language. The draft's struck passages are analytically important for absence analysis.

sources.title: Selected Washington General Orders and Circular Letters, 1775–1783
sources.citation: Washington, George. General Orders and Circular Letters, 1775–1783. Founders Online, National Archives. https://founders.archives.gov/
sources.rights_rationale: All Washington correspondence and orders are pre-1800 U.S. government and personal papers. Founders Online texts are public-domain transcriptions funded by the National Historical Publications and Records Commission. No copyright restriction applies.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Search Founders Online at https://founders.archives.gov/ filtering by author=Washington, document type=General Orders, date range 1775–1783. Select documents with high sacrificial or obligation language density based on a keyword pass for 'sacrifice', 'duty', 'liberty', 'blood', 'glory'. Target 15–25 orders for balanced-core inclusion. Download plain text. Document each selection with the Founders Online document ID in the manifest.
sources.notes: Military-order register is important for CMT register-control analysis. Washington's repeated use of sacrifice, duty, and glory language in orders is central to the corpus model. Selection must be documented to support cross-case register comparison.

sources.title: Jefferson's original Rough draught of the Declaration of Independence
sources.citation: Jefferson, Thomas. Original Rough draught of the Declaration of Independence, 1776. Founders Online, National Archives.
sources.rights_rationale: The underlying eighteenth-century document is public domain. Founders Online / National Archives provides citable scholarly metadata; modern annotations are not copied into raw corpus text.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Use Founders Online print view for primary text. Local CLI requests may receive an AWS WAF challenge; if so, acquire in browser and retain only primary text with provenance header.
sources.notes: Approved by the pre-v1 rights/provenance review for #180. Adds draft-stage evidence for final Declaration comparison and absence analysis.

sources.title: Address to the Officers of the Army
sources.citation: Washington, George. Address to the Officers of the Army, 15 March 1783. Founders Online, National Archives.
sources.rights_rationale: The underlying eighteenth-century document is public domain. Founders Online / National Archives provides citable scholarly metadata; modern annotations are not copied into raw corpus text.
sources.acquisition_instructions: Use Founders Online print view for primary text. Local CLI requests may receive an AWS WAF challenge; if so, acquire in browser and retain only primary text with provenance header.
sources.notes: Approved by the pre-v1 rights/provenance review for #180. Adds army-crisis and civil-military restraint evidence without replacing existing 1783 circular/order material.
